name: Marshal Royal
abstract: Marshal Walton Royal Jr. (December 5, 1912 – May 8, 1995) was an American jazz alto saxophonist and clarinetist best known for his work with Count Basie, with whose band he played for nearly 20 years.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Count Basie and His Orchestra
Newport Jazz Festival: Count Basie and His Orchestra
Kool Jazz Festival: Benny Carter and Illinois Jacquet
Kool Jazz Festival: Mel Torme and George Shearing
Kool Jazz Festival: A Salute to Count Basie

about_quotes.speaker: Nellie Lutcher
about_quotes.evidence: I've known him and worked with Marshall... We worked in San Pedro. We worked a little club in San Pedro. Alton Red was the leader and Marshall Royal was the saxophone player and I was the piano player.

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Capp
about_quotes.evidence: We had Marshall Royal in our band right up until he died a few months ago, right from the very first album
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Capp interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: The saxophones were Marshall Royal, Frank Wess, Ernie Wilkins, Charlie Fowlkes on baritone, and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.evidence: There was a dentist there that sounded like Marshall Royal on lead alto.

about_quotes.speaker: Buddy Collette
about_quotes.evidence: I was in the band with a bunch of the guys, you will probably know the names, Marshall Royal, Jerome Richardson, Jackie Kellso... We were stationed up at Oakland at the St. Mary’s pre-flight school.
about_quotes.doc_title: Buddy Collette interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 13, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: Personnel -- Marshall Royal, Harry Carney, Barney Bigard, Johnny Hodges, Tricky Sam Nanton, Juan Tizol... Do you remember making a short subject with Florence Mills and Bessie Dudley? For Paramount?
